behavior_summary: This script provides thin event-handler wrappers for the Tallon NPC that prepare context and delegate substantive behavior to shared sorcerer or spell/combat subsystems.
Handlers covered: U8P_TALLON::look (examine), use (interact), enterFastArea (area entry trigger), leaveFastArea (area exit trigger), and cast (spell targeted at Tallon).

Each wrapper typically sets an info id, applies a process-exclude/info operation on the actor/context, pushes the requesting pid and either a literal name or spell id, spawns an external handler (SORCERER::* or SPELCMBT::084D), evaluates the returned value with an 'implies' test, then suspends. The wrapper itself implements no substantive NPC logic.
notable_control_flow: All visible handlers are 'spawn' wrappers: they push context and constants, spawn an external routine, consume/free pushed resources, then apply an 'implies' test to the spawned routine's return and suspend.

Return values from the spawned handlers are used only in an 'implies' test (influence on control flow is limited to that implication), and then the script suspends — indicating final behavior and messaging is handled by the external routine.
Event-driven: each function corresponds to a distinct engine event (U8P_TALLON::look/use/enter/leave/cast) rather than being invoked by other script functions.
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of the numeric info ids (0x0207, 0x0208, 0x021B, 0x021C, 0x021D) and the indirect data block pushed before spawning — these are inferred to be metadata/info keys but their concrete game meanings are unknown.
Precise role of the 'implies' test and what control/state it affects after the spawn (beyond consuming the return and suspending).
Whether [BP+06h] is strictly 'self' (the Tallon actor) or the requesting actor/process in every event (the pattern suggests actor/context but could vary by event).
What values are represented by the pushed 'pid' in each handler (likely the requesting process/player PID) and how it influences external handlers' behavior.
Detailed behavior implemented in the external SORCERER::* handlers and SPELCMBT::084D (only their interfaces/purposes are visible here).

main_function.name: U8P_TALLON::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the 'U8P_TALLON::look' (examine) event for the Tallon NPC by preparing context (info id, actor context, pid, name) and delegating the display/response logic to the shared handler SORCERER::039C. After the handler returns, run an 'implies' test on its return and then suspend.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_TALLON::look) — triggered when the player or relevant actor examines Tallon (invokes the U8P_TALLON::look handler).

function_map.name: U8P_TALLON::look
function_map.role: Event handler for 'U8P_TALLON::look' that delegates to SORCERER::039C after setting info/context

function_map.name: U8P_TALLON::use
function_map.role: Event handler for 'use' that delegates to SORCERER::05EC after preparing context

function_map.name: U8P_TALLON::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area entry trigger handler that delegates to SORCERER::258C

function_map.name: U8P_TALLON::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Fast-area exit trigger handler that delegates to SORCERER::25D2

function_map.name: U8P_TALLON::cast
function_map.role: Spell-cast handler that forwards a spell id and context to SPELCMBT::084D

function_map.name: SORCERER::039C
function_map.role: Shared external handler that implements the substantive 'U8P_TALLON::look' behavior for sorcerer-type NPCs

function_map.name: SORCERER::05EC
function_map.role: Shared external handler that implements the 'use' behavior for sorcerer-type NPCs

function_map.name: SORCERER::258C
function_map.role: Shared external handler for fast-area entry behavior for sorcerer-type NPCs

function_map.name: SORCERER::25D2
function_map.role: Shared external handler for fast-area exit behavior for sorcerer-type NPCs

function_map.name: SPELCMBT::084D
function_map.role: Spell/combat subsystem routine handling spell effects targeted at Tallon

external_calls.purpose: Perform the actual 'U8P_TALLON::look' response for Tallon (receives info id 0x0208, actor/context, an indirect data block, pid, string 'Tallon', and actor/context).
Perform the 'use' interaction logic for Tallon (receives info id 0x0207, actor/context, indirect data, pid, string 'Tallon', actor/context).
Handle enter-area behavior for Tallon (receives info id 0x021B, actor/context, indirect data, pid, actor/context).
Handle leave-area behavior for Tallon (receives info id 0x021C, actor/context, indirect data, pid, actor/context).
Resolve a spell cast at Tallon by forwarding the spell id (from [BP+0Ah]) plus actor/context and other pushed values to the spell/combat subsystem.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script provides thin event-handler wrappers for the Tallon NPC that prepare context and delegate substantive behavior to shared sorcerer or spell/combat subsystems.
